title: DEML Completeness Investigation
author: Monogate Research
verdict: neg(x) = -x is not constructible in DEML. exp(+x) is not constructible in DEML. DEML is a proper subset of EML in expressive power.

key_identity.algebraic_proof: exp(-1) - ln(exp(-x) - ln(1)) = exp(-1) - ln(exp(-x)) = exp(-1) - (-x) = 1/e + x
key_identity.significance: DEML can recover x with a positive constant offset 1/e. This is the only linear-in-x term DEML generates. It always has slope +1, never -1.

target_neg_x.note: Error not converging to 0. This is evidence of non-approximability, not just non-representability.

constant_zero.note: 0 appears to be in the DEML closure as a limit (rapidly converging). Not exactly representable by a finite tree.

structural_proof.key_lemma: For any DEML tree T over {1, x}: the linear component of T(x) has slope in {0, +1} only.
proof: Case 1: x in left branch only. T = deml(f(x), c) = exp(-f(x)) - ln(c). The exp(-f(x)) term decays exponentially. Slope is negative and approaches 0.
Case 2: x in right branch via deml(c, deml(x,1)). By the key identity, this equals exp(-c) + x. Slope = +1 regardless of c.
Case 3: Composition of Case 1 and 2. The linear component always has slope +1 (from Case 2 terms) or is a decaying correction.
Consequence: No DEML tree has slope -1 at any x. Therefore neg(x) = -x is not DEML-constructible.
Corollary: exp(+x) = deml(neg(x), 1) is not DEML-constructible.
structural_proof.additional_observation: deml(x + 1/e, 1) = exp(-1/e) * exp(-x). Feeding the linear output back into the left branch produces exponential DECAY, not exponential growth. This is the structural trap: positive slopes loop back to decaying functions.

what_deml_generates: x + K for any DEML-constructible constant K [3 nodes: deml(K, deml(x,1))]
ln(x) + K via deml(K, x) = exp(-K) - ln(x), giving -ln(x) + constant
what_deml_cannot_generate: add(x, y) = x + y [requires neg for the general case]
mul(x, y) = x * y [requires exp(+x) and ln]

theorem_statement: THEOREM (DEML Incompleteness): The DEML operator deml(x,y) = exp(-x) - ln(y) with terminal 1 is strictly less expressive than EML. Specifically, exp(+x) is not DEML-constructible, and neg(x) = -x is not DEML-constructible. Proof: structural (slope argument) + exhaustive verification to N=17 nodes (861,952 trees).
r2_revised_scope: R2 no longer proves DEML completeness. R2 should instead: (1) formalize the DEML incompleteness theorem with a clean proof, (2) characterize the DEML closure precisely, (3) identify what minimal extension of DEML achieves completeness.
r3_scope_unchanged: R3 (DEML-aware BEST routing) is still valid. DEML adds exp(-x) as a 1-node primitive. The routing update is an engineering result, not a completeness claim.
